text:
Expected: SKILL.md describes the built page's offline behavior honestly — core viewing works offline, but the viewer JS lazily imports mermaid from cdn.jsdelivr.net for an optional export/preview feature, so 'fully offline-viewable' needs that qualification.
Actual: SKILL.md line 11 (and the intro) claims the output is 'fully offline-viewable' with no mention of the lazy CDN import, overstating the guarantee (found by the judge panel via grep on a built lirbox-c4.html).
suggestedCriterion: SKILL.md qualifies the offline claim: core viewing offline, optional export/preview feature lazily loads mermaid from a CDN.

text:
Expected: scripts/likec4.sh maps the host user into the container (e.g. --user "$(id -u):$(id -g)") so build output under dist/ is owned by the invoking user on Linux hosts, keeping SKILL.md step 4's 'mv dist/index.html' and dist/ cleanup working without sudo.
Actual: the docker run line has no --user mapping; on Linux the container writes dist/ as root and step 4 fails without sudo. Works on macOS (Docker Desktop uid-maps the bind mount), but the skill is published for general use.
suggestedCriterion: likec4.sh passes a --user mapping (host uid:gid) to docker run, and the smoke check still passes on macOS.
